reviews.review_description: HUD and interaction works great, the graphics....oh boy. the water still looks weird even after update, the shadow settings is a shame. nothing was done other than make the quest controller works, its a very lazy port, you can clearly see the professionalism right at the beginning  when the 144p low resolution FERAL logo show up. still a good game, its basically Tropico 4 with all DLC included.

Not sure what all the low reviews are about other than graphic issues and i tend to think.
these have been fixed.  great game.

I’m happy. The first release was really painful for the eyes but now it’s playable 😃 
Thanks 😊
Graphics are horrible, movement is dizzying, game is basicly unplayable! 30$ for a piece of junk. They won't give me a refund even though I haven't even played for an hour. GIVE ME A REFUND!!!

reviews.review_title: Update: Where did the day go??
reviews.review_description:
Update: Developers enabled anti-aliasing for Quest 3. That makes the game so much easier on the eyes. It’s time to get back into it, now! Of course, sharpening the main menu text would help, as well. Also, small ask, but can you figure out why changing the render resolution in QGO crashes the game once it starts to render a map?

—————————
This is a full featured city sim. 4 hours and several batteries later, I’m still not wanting to set this down. It definitely needs some visual tweaks. Higher res for text, distance (maybe for Quest 3). The water shaders render differently in each eye. Also, the turn controls need better distinction from the altitude controls. 

However, I’m going to spend many happy weeks with this full-featured sim.

I love Tropico and even though the graphics on this version aren’t that great, I was so excited to try this. It’s an absolute let down, trying to place roads correctly is an absolute nightmare. Don’t even bother trying to place them uphill, the terrain deforms and glitches through them. If the road placement worked, it would probably be something I would play for hours on end. Such a shame.

Have been a Tropico fan since it's release on console platforms, so upon its VR release felt compelled to try it.
I am loving the VR concept of it's release and eagerly await future developments.  It's always fun to "go to ground level" and wander through the town created.
The main bugs I have encountered are roads not connecting properly - if I notice builders or teamsters not driving to a location then check if roads are connected properly (intersections not forming properly etc.) In which case I delete a section of road and reconnect.
And occasionally the main line quest objectives in a level don't appear requiring a level to be restarted - this occurred earlier on when I played, but not too much lately.
The game-play has been streamlined compared to play on the console (no pesky research points to accumulate) which for me really improves the playability - city development can occur alot faster.
I can thoroughly recommend this game! There are alot of hours of game play involved between the main campaign and modern times campaign.
One recommendation I can't stress enough is make sure the roads connect properly...they should look like continuous tracts, if you see a "smear" pasted over a previously constructed road, delete and reconnect.
Enjoy!

It is blurred pixled in the game not enjoyable and the water don't look at it will mess with your eyes I gota refund for now that's not enjoyable to me and I like tropico games but even the quest game optimizer couldn't make the blurr go away...please fix and I'll get it after its fixed sorry devs 8 mins in and eyes was hurting

developer_response.body:
Thanks for your purchase of Tropico.

In order for us to investigate any issues with graphics, please contact our support team via Main Menu > Help & Support > Contact Support. This will provide us with more information about the device being used as well as the settings that you are seeing. Please provide a description of any issues and we'll be happy to investigate further.

reviews.review_title: Could be a good Tropico game…
reviews.review_description:
… if all the annoying issues will be fixed.
This is the full Tropico 4 game (yes, the one from 2011) ported to VR, with some great potential but many immersion breaking bugs & problems.

The good:
- Finally a proper city sim in VR.
- Almost infinite replayability.
- Being El Presidente is always fun.
The bad:
- Looks horrible, visibility distance and texture quaility (when zoomed out, like how you actually play) is horrible.
- The water is somehow out of sync and looking at it for some time can cause mild VR sickness.
- Some additions to the original game makes no sense: buildings snap to roads but become out of grid doing so, the new road tool with additional nodes messes up the road planning.

I really hope the devs keep maintaining & updating this game and it’s not just a money grab. Even the 3 stars feel a bit much for the current state, but wanted to emphasize the potential to become great.

You're getting the Full 100+ hour Tropico 4 game and DLCs. There's so much content, and the VR controls are pretty good. I'm having fun playing the game. 

But it has 1 major downside - the gane resolution (playing on Quest3). This looks like playing through the old Rift CV1; things in the distance are blurry. I've noticed it also seems to have some sort of dynamic resolution,  it'll look sharp for a moment then look blurry again. 

Things that would make it a 5 Star game. 
-- Improve the resolution for Quest3 headsets. I see many of the items cast shadows,  perhaps remove shadows and use the performance savings to increase resolution. 
-- Mixed Reality. I like how the menu screen is in Mixed Reality. What about featuring Mixed Reality during the main game mode? An option to Replace the sea/sky with Mixed Reality. Just an idea.
-- I experienced 1 bug with it crashing when changing your avatar name during the avatar creation screen. 


Tropico VR is still a good game in my books, but has the potential to be a great experience with a couple more patches.

Tropico 4 was one of my favorite city builders on the PC.  It was with heavy heart that I had to conclude - it's just not release-ready on the Quest3.  So I requested a refund.  If I hear of this changing, I'll re-buy.

1. Graphics are horrendous.  The level of aliasing of 3d lines is unforgivable.  Games with significantly more complicate graphics look far nicer.  This is true up close, but dramatically worse at a distance.
2. The 3d transform is buggy as heck.  Specifically, the left-eye and right-eye view of the ocean is out-of-sync.  This was the smaller issue, but the larger reason for my refund request.  The "cheap optical illusion" feeling it gave my eyes led me to become disoriented quickly.

I wanted to spend 8 hours on Tropico.  I barely made 18 minutes before I realized I had to request a refund.  Please fix these issues and let me know, and you'll get an instant re-buy.  Till then, I just can't trust the game is ready (or will ever be).

(Note: Second star was entirely based on how good the original game was)
The games is great, humorous deep and I enjoy it a lot! However the graphics literally make my eyes bleed.

reviews.review_description: I’d love to try to play, but I have horrible right stick drift and it is making the screen move all over the place. Devs, I beg you. Please add a movement system that doesn’t require the stick or at least let me change the controls. I would love to dive into a rich city manager, but I can’t if my screen moves constantly without me inputting anything.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for your purchase of Tropico.

If you have stick drift this will be an issue with the hardware. Please refer to the Meta guide to troubleshoot any issues with the controllers:

https://www.meta.com/help/quest/articles/fix-a-problem/troubleshoot-headsets-and-accessories/touch-controller-drift/

reviews.review_description: A full Tropico game in Vr!

This is an excellent city/politics sim but definitely needs some bug fixes. I'm currently stuck on Olympic heist. Replayed 4 times now, and mission objectives have failed to appear each time (at different points in progress), bringing progress to a screeching halt. I really hope the devs are still working on this and not leaving us with a broken game.
If you liked it on the flat screen.... I'm sold.
The water and the leaves in the trees 🌳 give this game a terrible look.  just bought it. Regretting it already

developer_response.body: An update to resolve any graphical issues is on the way soon. If you need assistance with anything else please email our support team via support@feralinteractive.com

reviews.review_description: What a great game! I have spent hours playing it and it’s been fantastic fun! I would rate this as a 5 star game easily but sadly the graphics are the worst I’ve seen in Oculus. With some development this game has got some seriously great and addictive game play

I’ll admit, when I first started playing I was off put by the graphics a bit, but nearly 5 hours later and I’m absolutely hooked lol. I’ve played all the city sims on quest, and this is by far the best imo!

I am not a review writing type of person. This is the first I have ever done. This is also my first Tropico game, and i understand it has quite a historical fan following. So I really feel sad for this game, so much potential.  Replayability is there, it really is fun and addictive, so much potential. Everything that others have said before me about the graphics and visibility, im sorry, is just shameful!!! 2024 amd and the graphics feel like something out of 1990s... think Sims 1. Should have gone for a later release date and gave this game the respect it deserved. It feels cheap and pushed. I am really hoping there will be patches... and soon. 
Also, there are some glitches in the interface that should be addressed. Like I said, if you can get over the glitches and horrible graphics, it really is a very fun game, great premise, it has promise. Please patch soon and give it the love it deserves.
